title: An R6 Class to represent a ZIPLNfit — ZIPLNfit
text: function ZIPLN() fits model instance object class ZIPLNfit. class comes set R6 methods, useful end-user exported S3 methods. See documentation coef(), sigma(), predict(). Fields accessed via active binding changed user.

title: An R6 Class to represent a ZIPLNfit — ZIPLNfit
text: Covariates Zero-Inflation parameter (using logistic regression model) can specified formula RHS using pipe (~ PLN effect | ZI effect) separate covariates PLN part model Zero-Inflation part. Note different covariates can used part.

title: An R6 Class to represent a ZIPLNfit — ZIPLNfit
text: n number samples/sites q number dimensions latent space p number variables/species d number covariates PLN part d0 number covariates ZI part nb_param_zi number parameters ZI part model nb_param_pln number parameters PLN part model nb_param number parameters ZIPLN model model_par list matrices parameters found model (B, Sigma, plus others depending variant) var_par list two matrices, M S2, estimated parameters variational approximation optim_par list parameters useful monitoring optimization latent matrix: values latent vector (Z model) latent_pos matrix: values latent position vector (Z) without covariates effects offset fitted matrix: fitted values observations (model) vcov_model character: model used covariance (either "spherical", "diagonal", "full" "sparse") zi_model character: model used zero inflation (either "single", "row", "col" "covar") loglik (weighted) variational lower bound loglikelihood loglik_vec element-wise variational lower bound loglikelihood AIC variational lower bound AIC BIC variational lower bound BIC entropy Entropy variational distribution entropy_ZI Entropy variational distribution entropy_PLN Entropy Gaussian variational distribution PLN component ICL variational lower bound ICL criteria vector loglik, BIC, ICL number parameters

title: An R6 Class to represent a ZIPLNfit — ZIPLNfit
text: newdata data frame look variables predict. omitted, fitted values used. responses Optional data frame containing count observed variables (matching names provided data PLN function), assuming interest testing model. type Scale used prediction. Either "link" (default, predicted positions latent space), "response" (predicted average counts, accounting zero-inflation) "deflated" (predicted average counts, accounting zero-inflation using PLN part model). level Optional integer value level used obtaining predictions. Level zero corresponds population predictions (default responses provided) level one (default) corresponds predictions evaluating variational parameters new data. envir Environment prediction evaluated

text: formula object class "formula": symbolic description model fitted. data optional data frame, list environment (object coercible .data.frame data frame) containing variables model. found data, variables taken environment(formula), typically environment model called. subset optional vector specifying subset observations used fitting process. weights optional vector observation weights used fitting process. zi character describing model used zero inflation, either "single" (default, one parameter shared counts) "col" (one parameter per variable / feature) "row" (one parameter per sample / individual). covariates specified formula RHS (see details) parameter ignored. penalties optional vector positive real number controlling level sparsity underlying network. NULL (default), set internally. See PLNnetwork_param() additional tuning penalty. control list-like structure controlling optimization, default generated ZIPLNnetwork_param(). See associated documentation details.

title: Control of ZIPLNnetwork fit — ZIPLNnetwork_param
text: Helper define list parameters control ZIPLNnetwork fit. arguments defaults.

title: Control of ZIPLNnetwork fit — ZIPLNnetwork_param
code: ZIPLNnetwork_param(   backend = c("builtin", "nlopt"),   inception_cov = c("full", "spherical", "diagonal"),   trace = 1,   n_penalties = 30,   min_ratio = 0.1,   penalize_diagonal = TRUE,   penalty_weights = NULL,   config_post = list(),   config_optim = list(),   inception = NULL )

title: Control of ZIPLNnetwork fit — ZIPLNnetwork_param
text: backend optimization backend, either "builtin" (default, joint Newton (M,ψ,R), combined partial E-step maxit_ve = 1) "nlopt" (CCSAQ). "builtin" consistently finds better ELBO across penalty path cost slower. inception_cov Covariance structure used inception PLN: "full" (default), "diagonal" "spherical". Non-full structures now fully supported: inception_cov != "full", penalty grid built empirical covariance latent residuals M − X·B (full-rank proxy Σ), avoiding broken max_pen = 0 previously occurred diagonal/spherical. trace integer verbosity. n_penalties integer specifies number values penalty grid internally generated. Ignored penalties non NULL min_ratio penalty grid ranges minimal value produces sparse value multiplied min_ratio. Default 0.1. penalize_diagonal boolean: diagonal terms penalized graphical-Lasso? Default TRUE penalty_weights either single list p x p matrix weights (default: weights equal 1) adapt amount shrinkage pairs node. Must symmetric positive values. config_post list controlling post-treatments (optional bootstrap, jackknife, R2, etc.). See details config_optim list controlling optimizer (either "nlopt" "torch" backend). See details inception Set parameters initialization: default, model initialized multivariate linear model applied log-transformed data, formula one provided user. However, user can provide PLNfit (typically obtained previous fit), sometimes speeds inference.

title: Control of ZIPLNnetwork fit — ZIPLNnetwork_param

code: compute_PLN_starting_point(Y, X, O, w, method = c("LM", "GLM"))

title: Helper function for PLN initialization. — compute_PLN_starting_point
text: Y Response count matrix X Covariate matrix. Note initialization fail model matrix singular. O Offset matrix (log-scale) w Weight vector (defaults 1) method character: strategy used initialize B. Either "LM" (default, fast weighted log-linear regression) "GLM" (p independent Poisson GLMs, accurate complex unbalanced designs slower).

title: Helper function for PLN initialization. — compute_PLN_starting_point
text: named list starting values model parameter B variational parameters M S2 used iterative optimization algorithm PLN()

title: Helper function for PLN initialization. — compute_PLN_starting_point
text: B: estimated weighted LM (method = "LM", default) p independent Poisson GLMs (method = "GLM"). GLM option gives better B estimates factorial unbalanced designs cost p IRLS fits. M: initialized log((1 + Y) / exp(O)) (M_full X*B + M_res parameterization). S: initialized element-wise 1 / sqrt(2 + Y), approximate VE-step optimum Omega = . adapts automatically count levels: high S zero counts (high uncertainty), low S large counts.

title: Helper function for PLN initialization. — compute_PLN_starting_point
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(barents) Y <- barents$Abundance X <- model.matrix(Abundance ~ Latitude + Longitude + Depth + Temperature, data = barents) O <- log(barents$Offset) w <- rep(1, nrow(Y)) compute_PLN_starting_point(Y, X, O, w) compute_PLN_starting_point(Y, X, O, w, method = "GLM") } # }

what: Compute offsets from a count data using one of several normalization schemes — compute_offset
title: Compute offsets from a count data using one of several normalization schemes — compute_offset
text: Computes offsets count table using one several normalization schemes (TSS, CSS, RLE, GMPR, Wrench, TMM, etc) described literature.

title: Compute offsets from a count data using one of several normalization schemes — compute_offset
code: compute_offset(   counts,   offset = c("TSS", "GMPR", "RLE", "CSS", "Wrench", "TMM", "none"),   scale = c("none", "count"),   ... )

title: Compute offsets from a count data using one of several normalization schemes — compute_offset
text: counts Required. abundance count table, preferably dimensions names species columns. offset Optional. Normalization scheme used compute scaling factors used offset PLN inference. Available schemes "TSS" (Total Sum Scaling, default), "CSS" (Cumulative Sum Scaling, used metagenomeSeq), "RLE" (Relative Log Expression, used DESeq2), "GMPR" (Geometric Mean Pairwise Ratio, introduced Chen et al., 2018), Wrench (introduced Kumar et al., 2018) "none". Alternatively user can supply vector matrix offsets (see note specification user-supplied offsets). scale Either "none" (default) "count". offset normalized scale counts ? ... Additional parameters passed specific methods (now CSS RLE)

title: Compute offsets from a count data using one of several normalization schemes — compute_offset
text: offset = "none", NULL else vector length nrow(counts) one offset per sample.

title: Compute offsets from a count data using one of several normalization schemes — compute_offset
text: RLE additional pseudocounts type arguments add pseudocounts observed counts (defaults 0L) compute offsets using positive counts (type == "poscounts"). mimics behavior DESeq2::DESeq() using sfType == "poscounts". CSS additional reference argument choose location function used compute reference quantiles (defaults median Nature publication can set mean reproduce behavior functions cumNormStat* metagenomeSeq). Wrench two additional parameters: groups specify sample groups type either reproduce exactly default Wrench::wrench() behavior (type = "wrench", default) use simpler heuristics (type = "simple"). Note () CSS normalization fails median absolute deviation around quantiles become instable high quantiles (limited count variations within across samples) /one sample less two positive counts, (ii) RLE fails common species across samples (unless type == "poscounts" specified) (iii) GMPR fails sample share species samples. TMM code two libraries simplified adapted M. Robinson (edgeR:::.calcFactorTMM). final output however different one produced edgeR:::.calcFactorTMM intended used model (whereas need multiplied sequencing depths edgeR)

title: Compute offsets from a count data using one of several normalization schemes — compute_offset
text: Chen, L., Reeve, J., Zhang, L., Huang, S., Wang, X. Chen, J. (2018) GMPR: robust normalization method zero-inflated count data application microbiome sequencing data. PeerJ, 6, e4600 doi:10.7717/peerj.4600 Paulson, J. N., Colin Stine, O., Bravo, H. C. Pop, M. (2013) Differential abundance analysis microbial marker-gene surveys. Nature Methods, 10, 1200-1202 doi:10.1038/nmeth.2658 Anders, S. Huber, W. (2010) Differential expression analysis sequence count data. Genome Biology, 11, R106 doi:10.1186/gb-2010-11-10-r106 Kumar, M., Slud, E., Okrah, K. et al. (2018) Analysis correction compositional bias sparse sequencing count data. code: data(trichoptera) trichoptera <- prepare_data(trichoptera$Abundance, trichoptera$Covariate) nets <- PLNnetwork(Abundance ~ 1 + offset(log(Offset)), data = trichoptera) #>  #>  Initialization... #>  Adjusting 30 PLN with sparse inverse covariance estimation #> 	Joint optimization alternating gradient descent and graphical-lasso #> 	sparsifying penalty = 2.123258  	sparsifying penalty = 1.961192  	sparsifying penalty = 1.811496  	sparsifying penalty = 1.673226  	sparsifying penalty = 1.54551  	sparsifying penalty = 1.427542  	sparsifying penalty = 1.318579  	sparsifying penalty = 1.217933  	sparsifying penalty = 1.124969  	sparsifying penalty = 1.039101  	sparsifying penalty = 0.9597877  	sparsifying penalty = 0.886528  	sparsifying penalty = 0.81886  	sparsifying penalty = 0.7563572  	sparsifying penalty = 0.6986251  	sparsifying penalty = 0.6452996  	sparsifying penalty = 0.5960444  	sparsifying penalty = 0.5505489  	sparsifying penalty = 0.508526  	sparsifying penalty = 0.4697106  	sparsifying penalty = 0.433858  	sparsifying penalty = 0.400742  	sparsifying penalty = 0.3701537  	sparsifying penalty = 0.3419002  	sparsifying penalty = 0.3158032  	sparsifying penalty = 0.2916982  	sparsifying penalty = 0.2694332  	sparsifying penalty = 0.2488676  	sparsifying penalty = 0.2298717  	sparsifying penalty = 0.2123258  #>  Post-treatments #>  DONE! if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ stability_selection(nets) probs <- extract_probs(nets, crit = "StARS", format = "vector") probs } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Add edge attributes to graph using igraph net_stars <- getBestModel(nets, "StARS") g <- plot(net_stars, type = "partial_cor", plot=F) library(igraph) E(g)$prob <- probs[as_ids(E(g))] g } # }

title: Cow microbiome data set — microcosm
text: data gives evolution microbiota 45 lactating cows calving terms counts Amplicon Sequence Variants (ASV) various body sites. Three body sites (vagina, mouth, nose) addition milk 4 teats sampled 4 times points: 1 week calving (except milk), 1 month, 3 months 7 months calving. present reduced version original data consisting 880 samples sequencing depths ranging 1,001 71,881 reads per sample (see doi:10.1186/s42523-023-00252-w  details). ASVs prevalence dataset lower 5% filtered samples total depth (ASV filtering removed), resulting count table n = 880 samples p = 259 ASV mean proportion zeroes 90.3%.

title: Display various outputs (goodness-of-fit criteria, robustness, diagnostic) associated with a collection of network fits (either PLNnetworkfamily or ZIPLNnetworkfamily) — plot.Networkfamily
text: x R6 object class PLNnetworkfamily ZIPLNnetworkfamily type character, either "criteria", "stability" "diagnostic" type plot. criteria Vector criteria plot, selected among "loglik" (log-likelihood), "BIC", "ICL", "R_squared", "EBIC" "pen_loglik" (penalized log-likelihood). Default  c("loglik", "pen_loglik", "BIC", "EBIC"). used type = "criteria". reverse logical indicating whether plot value criteria "natural" direction (loglik - 0.5 penalty) "reverse" direction (-2 loglik + penalty). Default FALSE, .e use natural direction, scale log-likelihood. log.x logical: x-axis represented log-scale? Default TRUE. stability scalar: targeted level stability stability plot. Default .9. ... additional parameters S3 compatibility. used

text: Prepare data proper format use PLN model variants. function () merges count table covariate data frame comprehensive way (ii) computes offsets count table using one several normalization schemes (TSS, CSS, RLE, GMPR, Wrench, etc). function fails informative messages heuristics used sample matching fail.

title: Prepare data for use in PLN models — prepare_data
text: counts Required. abundance count table, preferably dimensions names species columns. covariates Required. covariates data frame, preferably row names. offset Optional. Normalization scheme used compute scaling factors used offset PLN inference. Available schemes "TSS" (Total Sum Scaling, default), "CSS" (Cumulative Sum Scaling, used metagenomeSeq), "RLE" (Relative Log Expression, used DESeq2), "GMPR" (Geometric Mean Pairwise Ratio, introduced Chen et al., 2018), Wrench (introduced Kumar et al., 2018) "none". Alternatively user can supply vector matrix offsets (see note specification user-supplied offsets). call Optional. execution environment set local error call. ... Additional parameters passed compute_offset()

PLNmodels 1.3.0-9010
text: New built-Newton optimizer (backend = "builtin") PLN, ZIPLN PLNPCA: envelope-theorem Newton steps strong Wolfe line search, dependency NLOPT. Substantially faster accurate nlopt large datasets full covariance. Backend defaults revisited package-wide, based extensive benchmarking: PLN keeps "nlopt" (now consistently faster thanks profiled = TRUE, see ); PLNnetwork ZIPLNnetwork now default "builtin", finds better optimum modest speed cost; ZIPLN keeps "builtin" default. four remain configurable via backend argument; see corresponding *_param() documentation trade-offs. torch backend now clearly marked experimental everywhere. Quality speed improvements: config_optim$profiled = TRUE now default full-covariance nlopt fits (faster, slightly better loglik); ZIPLN’s variational step now optimises (M, ψ, R) jointly via Newton instead sequentially; PLNPCA shares single SVD initialisation across ranks can warm-start pre-fitted PLNfit large ranks (inception/init_method, see ?PLNPCA_param); ZIPLN’s starting point longer relies pscl::zeroinfl (now internal LM + binomial GLM routine), much faster better starting point — pscl longer dependency. Fixed critical nlopt convergence bug affecting PLN/PLNPCA: ill-conditioned covariate scaling trigger XTOL stopping criterion iterations, well convergence. built-backend never affected; nlopt now also fixed via better parameter scaling.

title: PLNmodels 1.3.0-9010
text: Shared covariance abstraction: optimization machinery PLN’s covariance structures (full, diagonal, spherical, fixed) now expressed via small set C++ traits (CovTraitsBase covariance_pln.h) instead duplicated per structure. PLNPCA ZIPLN’s variational step now reuse machinery instead separate hand-rolled implementations, removing substantial amount duplicated code fixing minor inefficiencies along way (e.g. ZIPLN’s VE-step used treat precision matrix dense even diagonal/spherical covariance, unnecessary O(np^2) cost). Consistent C++ naming: exported optimizer functions across PLN, PLNPCA ZIPLN now follow {backend}_optimize_{structure} convention.

title: PLNmodels 1.3.0-9010
text: Uniform covariate normalization: normalize_covariates() helper (zero mean, unit variance per column) now applied consistently optimize() methods (PLN, PLNPCA, PLNnetwork, ZIPLN). makes nlopt XTOL criterion scale-invariant stabilises torch backend. Parallelism backend: future.apply::future_lapply replaced parallel::mclapply throughout (stability selection PLNnetwork / ZIPLNnetwork). Use options(mc.cores = N) set number cores. Bug fixes: PLNnetwork/ZIPLNnetwork’s inception (warm-start) model didn’t inherit ftol_em/maxit_em user’s config_optim, silently falling back defaults producing wrong penalty grid; PLNPCA rank-model objective used − Y use − Y ⊙ Z; various ZIPLN prediction/initialization fixes (#146, #149, #150, #152). microcosm data now included (#153, #154); AIC added PLN ZIPLN classes (#151); fixes (#155).
